text:
is a character from Fullmetal Alchemist the Movie: Conqueror of Shamballa.

Appearance

Noah a young Roma woman who has sandy brown skin and long, dark brown hair with two front braids. She is a few inches taller than Edward Elric, and is always seen wearing a dress of some sort.  

Noah is notably very similar in appearance to Rose of Lior. Both have brown skin, dark hair with similar fringe (albeit Rose's fringe is pink in colour) and while never seen together, seem to even be the same height when compared to Edward. Aside from physical similarities, both women are of minority ethnicities within their respective countries and both face persecution from said countries governments. While never directly stated, it seems they are intended to be each other's Parallel World Counterpart.

Personality

Noah is typically quiet and solemn. She is shown to be proud of her heritage and culture, but is desperate for a way out of her difficult life caused by Anti-Romani sentiment, which is very prevalent in the Weimar Republic (post-World War I Germany). 

This makes her amenable to aiding the Thule Society in reaching "Shamballa", despite what the group believes. Noah desires peace, a life without persecution, and is willing to do anything for this, believing the ends justify the means.

History

Ed and Alfons Heiderich meet Noah when they hitch a ride on a truck traveling to a fair near Munich. Noah is one of their fellow passengers and is with a group of fellow Romani women. She is introduced by them, but seems somewhat distant from them, and does not join them in song when they neared the fair. At the fair her companions betray Noah and sell her to representatives of the Thule Society. She flees when she realizes what has happened and tries to hide underneath a cloth covering Ed and Alfons' rocket on the truck they arrived in. Ed notices this as he was resting in the truck's bed and ends up saving her from her pursuers. He eventually takes Noah back to the house he was boarding at in Munich with Alfons. Despite the circumstances under which she started staying with them, Noah is accepted by both Alfons as well as their landlady, the parallel world counterpart of Gracia Hughes.

It is revealed that Noah has the unexplained ability to read the minds of others, becoming fascinated with Ed's world after witnessing his memories of it while he is asleep. Believing that she wouldn't be discriminated in Ed's world, Noah assists Dietlinde Eckhart and the Thule Society in opening The Gate of Truth to Shamballa based on information from Ed's memories. She cries out to him to take her with him when he flies into the Gate. Shortly after this, Noah relents once Ed points out the errors of her ways. She parts ways with the Elric Brothers to be with her people by the ending, sending them off with Scar and Lust’s counterparts. Mizushima confirms in the Japanese Actor commentary track in the Special Edition DVD and in the Oct. 2005 Animedia magazine that the reason why Noah wasn’t seen in the cart is because she separated from the Elrics to be with her people. 

Trivia

Interestingly, her name is spelt "Noah" in English despite that traditionally being a masculine name, although it is also rarely used as a feminine name as seen in English versions of the bible, being the name of one of the daughters of Zelophehad. The alternative spellings Noa and No'a are feminine names used in several languages and are significantly more common for use as female names.
Much later after the movie's release, "Noah" spelt with an "h" would actually enter the United States' top 1,000 most used baby names for girls in the year 2020, which is the first time "Noah" with that spelling has ranked within the top 1000 for girls in any country. As of 2024, Noah is still within the top 1,000 used baby names for girls in the USA. Despite this, it has been consistently beaten out by the usual feminine spelling of Noa, which entered the United States' top 1000 years earlier in 2014. Aside from the USA, "Noah" has yet to appear in any other country's top 1,000 names for baby girls, although Noa has appeared in several for decades. https://www.behindthename.com/name/noa-1/top/united-states?compare=Noah&type=percent 
Noah briefly appears in the OVA Chibi Party, but does not have a speaking role. However a drunk Winry Rockbell angrily confronts Ed over the misinterpreted relationship between him and Noah, using a spread from a magazine and a photo of the bedroom scene showing them in a compromising position when Noah read his mind as evidence. While this is mainly parodying tabloid gossip, it may have been poking fun at the dropped concept of a love triangle which was present in an older draft of the movie according to the Scenario Book.
Apart from potentially being each other's counterparts, Noah and Rose share some commonalities:
Both have worn long cloaks at some point. Rose had worn a long white cloak when she was dressed as the Holy Mother. Noah wore a long red Roma cloak when traveling to the festival in Munich, Germany.
Both have turned their backs on Ed to fulfill their own goals: Rose turned her back on Ed and Al to go with Cornello to help revive her dead boyfriend and Noah turned her back on Ed to go with Dietlinde Eckhart to open the Gate of Truth to go to Shamballa.
Both danced at some point in the series: Rose in the ballroom in the underground city, and Noah at Alfons' funeral
